column2: The allocation holder must give to the chief executive, within 7 days after the end of each month, a written return in the approved form for all quarry material removed by the holder, in the month.
The allocation holder shall keep a record of daily extractions on site at all times. This record shall be made available for inspection upon request of an Authorised Officer of this Department.
The nominated removal rate for this notice is: 550 cubic metres (m3) per year.
The allocation is confined to within the bed and banks of the Murray River.

Existing sand, gravel, clay or rock bars that create a natural storage in the bed of the watercourse shall not be lowered or disturbed in any way to prejudice the interests or rights of owners of adjacent or affected lands, or water entitlements given under the provisions of the Water Act 2000.
Removal of material must not be carried out within 200 metres of any public road, bridge culvert or railway crossing.
All batters must be no steeper than 1 vertical and 4 horizontal.

The surface area of the operation within the bed of the watercourse shall be left at an even longitudinal grade, smooth and free from holes and ridges or as required by an approved works or rehabilitation plan.

Material removed from the watercourse should be stored above known flood level and must only be returned to the watercourse in accordance with an operation plan approved by the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines.

All vegetation, scrub, grass, timber and debris removed from the area of operations is to be disposed of in accordance with an operations plan approved by the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines.

Removal of material and associated operations shall not cause erosion or damage to the bed or banks, the vegetation growing thereon, pumps, fences or structures within the watercourse, nor shall they cause unauthorised interference to the flow of the watercourse.
The allocation holder is to ensure that any person employed or contracted by the allocation holder in connection with this operation understands and complies with the provisions of this allocation.
The allocation holder must maintain current permission to traverse any property to access the allocation site.

Access to the removal site over banks to the bed of the river or lake is via existing tracks only. These tracks are to be maintained and will be subject of rehabilitation to a standard as directed by the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines.
Work is only allowed to be undertaken within the area defined on site by the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines. This area will be defined by the following parameters:

Downstream Limit - Adjacent to Lot 103 on Plan CWL2262 defined by:

A measuring device of a type approved by the chief executive to record the volume of Quarry Material removed from the approved works must be installed and maintained and operated to the satisfaction of the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines at the allocation holders expense for the duration of all removal activities authorised by this allocation.
The allocation holder must not remove Quarry Material under the authority of this allocation unless a measuring device to measure the volume of all Quarry Material removed is installed.
Removals are to commence from the downstream end of the deposit and progress in an upstream direction.

The allocation holder must not destroy or interfere with any native vegetation, with the exception of grasses, herbs, and saplings that are less than 1 metre in height, without prior written consent of by the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines.
Removal of material and associated operations is prohibited:

column1: below the water level prevailing at the time of operation;
below 1 metre above the elevation of the bed of the adjacent low flow channel;
within 3 metres of the stem or trunk of any native vegetation;
within 3 metres of the toe of the banks of the Murray River.

column2: Consolidated material comprising the bed and banks of the water course may only be interfered with at the access track and for the purpose of maintaining the access track. Any other interference with the consolidated material comprising the bed and banks of the watercourse is prohibited.

In the case of a screening process, reject oversize material and unwanted sand shall be replaced in the bed of the excavation over the removal area such that it smoothly transitions to the adjacent natural bed and banks.

Stockpiling of quarry material in the bed of the watercourse is not permitted from the 1st November to the 1st April except by written approval of by the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines.

Care shall be taken to disturb as little as possible any mud, clay or fine silt which could cause increased discolouration of the water or cause siltation in Murray River downstream of this operation.

The allocation holder must, to the satisfaction of the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines, ensure that removal of material and associated operations does not release acid and associated metal contaminants into the environment by:

column1: - monitoring works by the use of approved field testing methods; and
- not disturbing acid sulfate soils when excavating or otherwise removing soil or sediment; or
- treating and, if required, undertaking ongoing management of any disturbed  acid sulfate soils and drainage waters.

column2: The allocation holder must notify the chief executive of the completion of the approved works within 30 business days after such completion. The notification must be given in writing to the chief executive of the Department of Natural Resources and Mines.

A1. The allocation holder must give to the chief executive administering the Water Act 2000, within 7 days after the end of each month, a written return in the approved form for all quarry material, including nil extractions, removed by the holder, in the month.
A2. All quarry material removed under the authority of this allocation notice is identified as state owned, applicable royalties must be paid.
A3. The allocation holder must have an accurate means of measuring (in cubic meters) the volume of quarry material that is removed from the watercourse.

A4. The allocation holder must keep a daily record of the volumes of quarry material removed from the watercourse under the authority of this allocation notice. This record must be made available for inspection upon request by an Authorised Officer of the department administering the Water Act 2000.
A5. The volume of quarry material authorised by this allocation notice to be removed in each 12-month period commencing from 01 July to 30 June is 5,000 cubic metres.

A6. A copy of this allocation notice and the accompanying conditions pertaining to this notice must be available at the extraction area at all times when extraction of quarry material is occurring. All personnel undertaking the extraction of material must be made aware of, and understand, the requirements and conditions of this notice.
A7. Removal of material and associated operations must not â€“

a) Interfere with consolidated material comprising the bed and banks of the watercourse, except for the construction and maintenance of access tracks authorised by the chief executive of the department administering the Water Act 2000
b) Change the existing flow of the watercourse
c) Cause any batters to be steeper than 1 vertical on 3 horizontal
d) Cause an uneven longitudinal grade surface area within the bed of the watercourse
A8. Excavation of material and associated activities are prohibitedâ€“
a) Within 5 metres of the stem or trunk of any native vegetation greater than 1 metre in height.
b) Within 5 metres of the toe of the banks
c) Below the elevation of the bedrock terrace
d) Below the water level prevailing at the time of extraction
A9. Within the watercourse, access tacks to the authorised extraction area must not interfere with the flow of water or cause erosion.

A10. Excavated material must not be stockpiled within the watercourse between the 1st of November and the 1st of April except where approved by the chief executive of the department administering the Water Act 2000.

A11. Reject riverine material that is not removed from the watercourse, must be respread within the authorised extraction area, such that it smoothly transitions to the adjacent natural bed and banks.
Schedule B Conditions

B1. Photographs must be taken of the extraction area authorised under this allocation notice, prior to the expiry of this QMA, in accordance with the requirements outlined below: Photographs must show -
a) Access track/s at  extraction area/s within the watercourse; and
b) Bed and banks of water course of the extraction area; and
c) Looking upstream of the extraction area/s; and
d) Looking downstream of the extraction area/s.

B2. Photographs required by B1, must be taken within two (2) months prior to the date of the allocation notice expiry and include a description, date and coordinates of the location from which they were taken.

B3. Photographs required by B1 must be provided to the chief executive administering the Water Act 2000 prior to this allocation notice expiry date and if applicable in conjunction with any application for renewal.

â€¢ Within 30 metres of the toe of the banks of the Don River;
â€¢ Within 5 metres of the stem or trunk of any native vegetation;
â€¢ Within 10 metres of the low flow channel within the Don River;
â€¢ Within 100 metres of any public road, bridge culvert or railway crossing;
â€¢ Below 1 metre above the elevation of the bed of the adjacent low flow channel; and

â€¢ Rectified orthophotograph.
â€¢ An explanatory report in PDF format which includes:

buffer distances and maximum extraction depths defined by the QMAN conditions.

column1: 300310,Quarry Material Allocation Notice,BURNETT READYMIX PTY LTD,12 Mary Street, Gayndah, QLD 4625",136,Burnett Basin,136.01.21,Boyne River,South Burnett Regional,1/SP154257,Granted,45000,12350,32650,9000,28/02/2018,27/02/2028,Bundaberg,Sand,-26.39687,151.2963,,-26.41666,151.31755,,,"1. Within seven (7) days after the end of each month of
currency, the permittee must provide an accurate quarry return,
accompanied by a payment of prescribed royalties, for all
materials taken during that month. A nil return is required
for each month that no removals were made. The return is to be
provided to the chief executive at BUNDABERG and must be in
writing on the required forms.
2. The permit area is confined to within the bed and banks of
Boyne River
3. Existing sand, gravel, clay or rock bars that create a
natural storage in the bed of the watercourse shall not be
lowered or disturbed in any way to prejudice the interests or
rights of owners of adjacent or affected lands, or water
entitlements given under the provisions of the Water Act 2000.
4. Removal of material must not be carried out within 50 metres
of any public road, bridge culvert or railway crossing.
5. Removal of material and associated operations shall not
cause erosion or damage to the bed and banks, the vegetation
growing thereon, pumps, fences or structures within the
watercourse, nor shall they cause unauthorised interference to
the flow of the watercourse.
6. All batters must be no steeper than 1 vertical on 3
horizontal.
7. The surface area of the operation within the bed of the
watercourse shall be left at an even longitudinal grade, smooth
and free from holes and ridges.
8. Material removed from the watercourse should be stored above
known flood level.
9. Removals are not to occur within 10 metres from the toe of
the bank of the watercourse and no closer than 20 metres from
the low flow channel.
10. Material must not be removed from within 100m of waterholes."

A2. All quarry material removed under the authority of this allocation notice is identified as state owned, applicable royalties must be paid.
A3. The allocation holder must have an accurate means of measuring (in cubic meters) the volume of quarry material that is removed from the watercourse.

A4. The allocation holder must keep a daily record of the volumes of quarry material removed from the watercourse under the authority of this allocation notice. This record must be made available for inspection upon request by an Authorised Officer of this department administering the Water Act 2000.
A5. The volume of quarry material authorised by this allocation notice to be removed in each 12-month period commencing from 01 March to 28 February is 600 cubic metres.

A6. A copy of this allocation notice and the accompanying conditions pertaining to this notice must be available at the extraction area at all times when extraction of quarry material is occurring. All personnel undertaking the extraction of material must be made aware of, and understand, the requirements and conditions of this notice.
A7. Removal of material and associated operations must not â€“

a) Interfere with consolidated material comprising the bed and banks of the watercourse, except for the construction and maintenance of access tracks authorised by the chief executive of the department administering the Water Act 2000.
b) Change the existing flow of the watercourse.
c) Cause any batters to be steeper than 1 vertical on 3 horizontal.
d) Cause an uneven longitudinal grade surface area within the bed of the watercourse.
A8. Excavation of material and associated activities are prohibitedâ€“
a)
column2: Within 3 metres of the stem or trunk of any native vegetation greater than 2 metres in height.

column1: b)
column2: Within 3 metres of the toe of the bank.

column1: c)
column2: Below the water level prevailing at the time of extraction.

column1: A9. Within the watercourse, access tracks to the authorised extraction area must not interfere with the flow of water or cause erosion.

A10. Excavated material must not be stockpiled within the watercourse between the 1st of November and the 1st of April except where approved by the chief executive of the department administering the Water Act 2000.

A11. Reject riverine material that is not removed from the watercourse, must be respread within the authorised extraction area, such that it smoothly transitions to the adjacent natural bed and banks.

column1: 3. All batters must be no steeper than one (1) vertical on three (3) horizontal.
4. The surface area of the removal site shall be left at an even longitudinal grade, smooth and free from holes and ridges.
5. Access to the removal site may only be undertaken using existing access tracks.
6. Reject oversize material and unwanted sand removed under the authority of this allocation notice shall be replaced in the removal site on an even gradient over the removal area.
7. The allocation holder shall keep a record of daily extractions. This record shall be made available for inspection upon request of an officer of this Department.

8. The allocation holder must give to the chief executive, within 7 days after the end of each month, a written return on the approved form for all quarry material removed by the holder, in the month.
